summary: Updated the progress-reporting tool's trophy total from 290 to 293 in both the bytes-per-trophy calculation and printed progress message. The PR body notes the project was still at 22 trophies, implying the old denominator made the trophy-style progress metric inaccurate.
what_changed: In tools/calcprogress.py, changed bytesPerTrophy from dol_code_size / 290 to dol_code_size / 293.
Updated the user-facing progress string from "You have {} of 290 Trophies" to "You have {} of 293 Trophies".
No source decompilation, symbol, or matching changes were included; the diff is limited to the progress calculation script.
smart_moves: Kept the calculation denominator and displayed total in sync, avoiding a mismatch between computed trophyCount and the printed total.
Made a minimal targeted tooling correction with only the two necessary numeric updates.
decomp_lessons: Progress gamification constants in tooling should reflect the canonical game totals; even small denominator errors affect perceived decomp progress.
When correcting a progress metric constant, update both the internal calculation and any user-facing output that repeats the same value.
Small tooling fixes can matter for contributor motivation and accuracy, even when they do not affect matching or binary output.
naming_conventions: Existing camelCase local variable names in calcprogress.py were preserved, including bytesPerTrophy, trophyCount, bytesPerEvent, and eventCount.
No new names or decomp symbol naming conventions were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were involved; this PR only changed Python progress-tool constants.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/calcprogress.py to see whether trophy/event progress metrics were further revised.
Search for other hard-coded game totals in tooling that may need canonical verification.
